Container.Name: MediaLyze
Container.Overview: Self-hosted technical media analysis for large video and audio collections.
Container.Description:
MediaLyze scans local media libraries with ffprobe, stores normalized technical metadata in SQLite, and exposes inspection, statistics, and scan-management workflows through a web UI.

MediaLyze is designed for analysis, not playback or file modification. Mount your media libraries read-only.

  • /config: persistent configuration and SQLite database storage.
  • /media: MediaLyze media root. Mount each Unraid share as a separate folder below this path, for example /media/Media, /media/Movies, /media/Shows, or /media/Music. Do not mount one share directly to /media and another below it.
Container.Requires: Persistent storage for /config and read-only media mounts below /media.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: External port for the MediaLyze web interface.

Config.Name: Config
Config.Description: Persistent configuration and SQLite database storage.

Config.Name: Primary Media Mount
Config.Description: Required read-only media mount. Change the container path to your preferred visible name, but keep it below /media, for example /media/Movies.

Config.Name: Movies Media Mount
Config.Description: Optional additional read-only media mount. Change the container path to your preferred visible name, but keep it below /media, for example /media/Movies.

Config.Name: Shows Media Mount
Config.Description: Optional additional read-only media mount. Change the container path to your preferred visible name, but keep it below /media, for example /media/Shows.

Config.Name: Music Media Mount
Config.Description: Optional additional read-only media mount. Change the container path to your preferred visible name, but keep it below /media, for example /media/Music.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Container timezone, for example Europe/Berlin or America/New_York.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: Optional runtime user id. On Unraid, 99 is usually nobody.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Optional runtime group id. On Unraid, 100 is usually users.
